mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite a 2% increase in CPI over the past 6 months, or the correlation between CPI and S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 3 months indicates an increase in supply chain activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite a 2% increase in INDPRO over the past 3 months, or the correlation between INDPRO and S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Estate Sales (RSAFS) over the past 2 months indicates an increase in housing market activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative despite a 1% increase in RSAFS over the past 2 months, or the correlation between RSAFS and S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past month, combined with a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the past 6 months, signals an easing monetary cycle with rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < -0.5% over 1 month and CPIAUCSL > 1% over 6 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite a 0.5% decrease in FEDFUNDS and a 1% increase in CPIAUCSL, or the correlation between the interaction term and S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Nonfarm Payroll Employment (PAYEMS) over the past month, combined with a 0.2% decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months, signals a tightening labor market with rising employment, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 0.5% over 1 month and UNRATE < -0.2% over 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 2 months indicates an increase in housing market activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, but only during the late-cycle expansion regime.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative despite a 2% increase in HOUST over the past 2 months during the late-cycle expansion regime, or the correlation between HOUST and S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during this regime.
